document_id: eFile Corporate Approved Software Developer List - Tax Year 2020

findings.description: Table header row styling differs - PDF uses black background with white text, HTML uses plain styling with no background shading
findings.evidence: PDF: Black/dark header row with white text for column headers. HTML: Plain white/light background with dark text for headers.

findings.description: Title text formatting differs slightly - PDF has centered bold title, HTML has left-aligned title
findings.evidence: PDF: Title is centered on page. HTML: Title appears left-aligned.

findings.description: Alternating row shading in PDF table not reproduced in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF: Alternating grey/white row backgrounds. HTML: Uniform white background for all rows.

findings.description: Alternating row shading in PDF table not reproduced in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF: Alternating grey/white row backgrounds. HTML: Uniform white background for all rows.

findings.description: Text alignment in table cells differs - PDF has center-aligned text in most columns, HTML has left-aligned text
findings.evidence: PDF: Software Provider, Software Product Name, Support Payments columns are center-aligned. HTML: All columns appear left-aligned.

findings.description: Text alignment in table cells differs - PDF has center-aligned text, HTML has left-aligned text
findings.evidence: PDF: Table data is center-aligned. HTML: Table data is left-aligned.

findings.description: URLs in the HTML are rendered as plain text rather than as clickable hyperlinks. The PDF contains hyperlinks for URLs like https://www.wolterskluwer.com, sales@crosslinktax.com, sales@drakesoftware.com, www.hrblock.com, and jeff.russo@taxtechnologies.com, but the HTML does not wrap them in <a> tags.
findings.evidence: For example, 'https://www.wolterskluwer.com' and 'sales@crosslinktax.com' appear as plain text in <td> cells rather than as hyperlinks.

findings.description: The HTML contains duplicate nested <main> elements, which while not causing visible content duplication, is a structural error with two <main> tags nested inside each other.
findings.evidence: The HTML has <main> wrapping another <main> element: '<body><main><main>...</main></main></body>'
